name: Greek Shrimp Saganaki
description: A classic Greek dish of shrimp baked in a rich tomato sauce with feta cheese and herbs.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F and place a rack in the center of the oven. Use a large oven-safe skillet (10–12 inch) for best results.
Heat olive oil in the skillet over medium-low heat. Add the shallots and garlic and cook for 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened but not browned.
Add the diced tomatoes with their juices, salt, black pepper, cumin, red pepper flakes, and honey. Stir well and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and let the sauce simmer uncovered for 15–20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
Remove the skillet from heat and arrange the shrimp in a single layer over the sauce.
Sprinkle the feta cheese evenly over the shrimp, then sprinkle the dried oregano on top.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until the shrimp are pink, opaque, and fully cooked through.
Switch the oven to broil and cook for 1–2 minutes until the feta is lightly golden on top. Watch closely so it doesn’t burn.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with fresh mint before serving. Let sit for 2–3 minutes before serving.

name: French Fry Seasoning
description: A simple homemade spice blend that adds bold flavor to fries and other dishes.
instructions: Add all ingredients to a small mixing bowl.
Stir thoroughly for about 30–60 seconds until all spices are evenly combined and there are no clumps.
Transfer the seasoning to an airtight container or spice jar for storage.
To use, sprinkle over hot fries immediately after cooking so the seasoning sticks better.

name: Beef and Broccoli
description: A simple takeout-style beef and broccoli with a savory homemade sauce.

ingredients.name: carrots
ingredients.prepNote: halved and cut into long slices

instructions: Slice the steak into very thin strips (about 1/4 inch thick) against the grain so it stays tender when cooked.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, chili garlic sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, sherry, water, and cornstarch until smooth and fully combined.

Heat a 10–12 inch skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add about 1 tbsp oil. Once the oil is hot and shimmering, add the steak in a single layer and cook for 2–3 minutes per side until browned. Remove from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add a little more oil if needed, then add onion, carrots, and garlic. Cook for 2–3 minutes until slightly softened and fragrant.
Add the broccoli and cook for another 3–5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until bright green and slightly tender but still crisp.
Pour the sauce into the pan and bring to a simmer over medium heat. Cook for 2–3 minutes until the sauce begins to thicken.
Return the steak to the pan and stir everything together. Cook for another 2–3 minutes until the beef is fully cooked and the sauce coats everything evenly.
Remove from heat and let sit for 2–3 minutes before serving so the sauce thickens slightly more. Serve hot over rice if desired.

name: Chimichurri Sauce
description: A bright, fresh herb sauce perfect for grilled meats with garlic, vinegar, and olive oil.
instructions: Remove any thick stems from the parsley and basil, then rinse the herbs in a colander and shake off excess water.
Finely chop the parsley, basil, garlic, and onion using a sharp knife or food processor. Do not over-blend—the mixture should be finely chopped, not a paste.
Place all chopped ingredients into a medium mixing bowl. Add olive oil, balsamic vinegar, lemon juice, paprika, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Stir everything together thoroughly until well combined. The mixture should look loose and oily with evenly distributed herbs.
Let the sauce sit at room temperature for at least 10–15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to blend together.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed by adding more salt, vinegar, or oil depending on preference.
Serve immediately over grilled meat, chicken, or vegetables, or store in the refrigerator and bring back to room temperature before using.

name: Mongolian Beef
description: A quick takeout-style beef dish with a sweet and savory garlic soy sauce.
instructions: Slice the flank steak into very thin pieces about 1/4 inch thick, cutting against the grain so the meat stays tender. Place the slices in a bowl and toss with cornstarch until evenly coated, then shake off any excess.
Heat a 10-inch skillet over medium-low heat and add 2 tsp vegetable oil. Once warm, add the ginger and garlic and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant but not browned.

Add soy sauce, water, and brown sugar to the skillet. Stir well and bring to a gentle boil over medium heat. Let it cook for 3–5 minutes until the sauce slightly thickens, then remove from heat and set aside.

Heat a separate 10–12 inch skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add the remaining oil. Once the oil is hot (shimmering but not smoking), add the beef in small batches so the pan is not overcrowded.
Cook each batch for about 2 minutes, flipping once, until lightly browned but not fully cooked through. Remove and repeat with remaining beef.
Return all the cooked beef to the pan and pour the prepared sauce over it. Cook over medium heat for 2–3 minutes, stirring, until the sauce becomes thick and coats the beef evenly.
Remove from heat and stir in the sliced green onions. Serve immediately while hot.

name: Doctored Cake Mix Cake
description: A simple upgraded cake mix recipe that turns a boxed mix into a richer, more moist cake.

ingredients.name: instant espresso granules, dissolved in 1/2 cup warm water

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F and grease two 9-inch round cake pans or a 9x13 inch baking dish.
In a large mixing bowl, add the cake mix, pudding mix, sour cream, vegetable oil, eggs, vanilla extract, and espresso mixture.
Use a hand mixer or stand mixer on medium speed for about 2 minutes until the batter is smooth and fully combined.
Pour the batter evenly into the prepared pans and spread it out with a spatula so the tops are level.
Place the pans in the center rack of the oven and bake for 25–30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and let the cakes cool in the pans for about 10–15 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely before frosting or serving.

name: Mushroom Spinach Crustless Quiche
description: A simple, protein-packed quiche with sautéed vegetables and cheese—great for breakfast or meal prep.

ingredients.name: olive oil, plus more for greasing pan

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F and lightly grease a 9-inch pie dish with olive oil. Place the dish on a baking sheet to catch spills.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and milk until fully combined and slightly frothy, about 30–45 seconds.

Heat a 10–12 inch skillet over medium-high heat and add 1 tbsp olive oil. Once the oil is shimmering, add the mushrooms, onion, and garlic. Cook for 10–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms release their moisture and the onions become soft and translucent.
Add thyme, salt, and black pepper to the skillet, then drizzle in the balsamic vinegar and stir. Cook for another 1–2 minutes.
Add the spinach and cook for about 2–3 minutes, stirring until fully wilted and reduced in size. Remove from heat.
Spread the cooked vegetable mixture evenly into the prepared pie dish. Sprinkle the cubed cheese over the top.
Pour the egg mixture evenly over everything, filling the dish close to the top.
Carefully place the baking sheet in the oven and bake for 35 minutes, or until the center is set and no longer jiggles when gently shaken.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before slicing so it holds its shape better.

name: Slow Cooker Korean Beef
description: Tender, shredded beef cooked low and slow in a sweet, savory, and slightly spicy Korean-inspired sauce with garlic, ginger, and gochujang. (Prep time not clearly stated)

ingredients.name: Beef Chuck Roast
ingredients.prepNote: Fat trimmed and cut into 1.5" pieces

instructions: In a 6-quart slow cooker, add the ginger, garlic, beef broth, brown sugar, soy sauce, gochujang, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and cornstarch. Whisk everything together directly in the slow cooker until smooth and fully combined.
Cut the beef chuck roast into large chunks (about 2–3 inches each) and place them into the slow cooker. Turn the pieces using tongs so they are fully coated in the sauce.
Cover with the lid and cook on HIGH for 5–6 hours or until the beef is very tender and easily pulls apart with a fork.
Once cooked, turn off the slow cooker and let it sit uncovered for about 10–15 minutes so the sauce can settle and cool slightly.
Use a spoon to skim off excess fat from the surface of the sauce, leaving a small amount for flavor.
Remove the beef and place it on a cutting board or large bowl. Use two forks to shred the meat into bite-sized pieces.
Return the shredded beef to the slow cooker and stir it into the sauce until evenly coated.
Let it sit in the warm sauce for 5–10 minutes so it absorbs more flavor.
Serve warm over rice or with vegetables, spooning extra sauce over the top.

name: Slow Cooker Flank Steak
description: A tender, flavorful flank steak slow-cooked in a savory-sweet marinade—perfect for an easy, hands-off dinner.
instructions: In a small mixing bowl, combine the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, honey, Worcestershire sauce, Dijon mustard, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper. Whisk for about 30–45 seconds until fully combined.
Place the flank steak into a 6-quart slow cooker (or similar size). Pour the marinade over the steak. Use tongs or a spoon to flip the steak 1–2 times so it is fully coated in the liquid.

Cover the slow cooker with the lid. Cook on LOW for 6 hours (recommended for most tender results) or HIGH for 3–4 hours. The steak is done when it is very tender and can be easily pulled apart with a fork.
Carefully remove the steak from the slow cooker and place it on a cutting board. Let it rest for 5–10 minutes so the juices redistribute and the meat stays moist.
Using a sharp knife, slice the steak against the grain into thin strips (this makes it much more tender and easier to chew).
Serve warm with the juices from the slow cooker spooned over the top for extra flavor.

name: Cowboy Caviar
description: A fresh, colorful bean and vegetable dip tossed in a zesty homemade dressing—great as a dip with chips or as a side salad.
instructions: Rinse and drain the black beans and black eyed peas thoroughly using a colander. Shake off excess water so the dip doesn’t become watery.
Dice the tomatoes, jalapeño, bell pepper, and red onion into small, even pieces (about ¼-inch size). Chop the cilantro and avocado last so it stays fresh.
In a large mixing bowl (at least 3–4 quarts), combine the beans, black eyed peas, tomatoes, jalapeño, corn, bell pepper, red onion, and cilantro.
In a small bowl, add the olive oil, lime juice, honey, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper. Whisk for about 30 seconds until fully combined and slightly thickened.
Pour the dressing over the bean mixture. Use a large spoon to gently toss everything together until evenly coated.
Add the chopped avocado and gently fold it in last to avoid smashing it.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed (add more salt, lime juice, or spice depending on preference).
Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving to let the flavors blend together (optional but recommended).
Serve chilled or at room temperature in a large bowl with tortilla chips, or use as a topping for grilled meats.

name: Fried Cornmeal Mush
description: A simple, old-fashioned dish made from cornmeal that’s chilled, sliced, and fried until crispy on the outside and soft inside—often served with butter or syrup. (Prep time not clearly stated) (Cook time not clearly stated — includes chilling time)
instructions: Lightly grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan (or similar small baking dish) and set aside.
In a medium saucepan (about 2–3 quarts), bring 2¾ cups of water to a boil over medium-high heat. You should see steady bubbling, not just light steam.
In a bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, 1 cup cold water, salt, and sugar until completely smooth with no lumps.

Once the water is boiling, slowly pour in the cornmeal mixture while stirring constantly with a spoon or whisk. Keep the heat at medium and stir continuously to prevent lumps. The mixture will begin to thicken within 2–3 minutes.
When it reaches a thick, smooth, porridge-like consistency, reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring every 1–2 minutes to prevent sticking or burning on the bottom.

Pour the thickened mixture into the prepared loaf pan and spread it evenly using a spatula. Let it cool at room temperature for about 15–20 minutes, then transfer to the refrigerator for at least 2 hours, or until completely firm. It should feel solid and hold its shape when touched.
Remove from the pan and slice into ½ to 1-inch thick pieces using a sharp knife. Try to keep slices even so they cook evenly.
Heat a 10–12 inch skillet over medium heat and add the butter. Once the butter is melted and lightly bubbling (but not browning), add the slices in a single layer.
Cook each side for 4–5 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy. Flip carefully using a spatula. The outside should be crisp while the inside stays soft.
Serve warm with maple syrup, butter, or bacon bits if desired.

name: Corn Chowder
description: A creamy, hearty corn chowder loaded with sweet corn, tender potatoes, and bacon in a rich, flavorful broth.
instructions:
Prep the corn: 
Cut the kernels off the corn cobs using a knife. Set the kernels aside and keep the empty cobs—you’ll use them for extra flavor.

Make the base broth:
In a large pot, add the chicken broth, milk, and heavy cream. Place the corn cobs into the liquid and bring it to a gentle simmer over medium heat. Let it cook for about 15–20 minutes to infuse flavor, then remove and discard the cobs.

Cook the bacon:
In a separate large pot or Dutch oven, cook the bacon over medium heat until crispy (about 5–7 minutes). Remove the bacon and place it on a paper towel to drain, leaving the fat in the pot.

Sauté the vegetables:
Dice the onion, carrot, and celery into small pieces. Add them to the pot with the bacon fat and cook over medium heat for about 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened and slightly translucent.

Add potatoes and corn:
Peel and dice the potatoes into bite-sized chunks. Add them to the pot along with the corn kernels. Stir everything together.

Season and combine:
Add salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper. Pour in the prepared broth mixture. Stir well to combine.

Simmer until tender:
Bring the soup to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and let it simmer uncovered for about 15–20 minutes, or until the potatoes are fork-tender (you should be able to easily pierce them).

Finish and serve:
Chop the cooked bacon into small pieces. Taste the soup and adjust seasoning if needed. Serve hot, topped with bacon and chopped chives.

name: Beef Stew
description: This classic beef stew recipe is hearty, versatile, and freezer-friendly—a cozy one-pot meal that warms you from the inside out.

ingredients.name: boneless beef chuck, (well-marbled, cut into 1½-inch pieces)
large carrots, (peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks on a diagonal)
small white boiling potatoes ((baby yukons), cut in half)

instructions: Preheat oven (325) and season beef with salt and pepper.
Brown beef in batches in oil; remove and set aside.
Cook onions, garlic, and balsamic vinegar; stir in tomato paste.
Return beef, add flour, and stir.
Add wine, broth, water, and seasonings; bring to a boil.
Cover and cook in oven for about 2 hours.
Add carrots and potatoes.
Cook 1 more hour until tender.

name: Chicken Tetrazzini
description: My Chicken Tetrazzini recipe will quickly become one of your favorite casseroles! It's creamy and cheesy, and so easy to make. I made it even easier by using rotisserie chicken, but you'll find directions on how to swap baked and chopped chicken in the notes below. This recipe is freezer-friendly and can be made ahead by storing it in the fridge before baking.

ingredients.name: rotisserie chicken (shredded or diced, or cook 1.5 lbs of chicken breast*)
half & half (or sub with equal parts milk; heavy cream)

instructions: Preheat Oven to 350˚F and butter a 9x13 Casserole dish.

Cook pasta in a large pot of salted boiling water (it should taste like ocean water) and cook until barely al dente according to package instructions - do not overcook. Reserve 1/2 cup of pasta cooking water, then drain the pasta, partially cover, and set aside.

Saute the Veggies: Place a large skillet over medium/high heat. Add oil then add sliced mushrooms and sauté 3 min or until softened. Add diced onions and cook until onions are soft and golden, 5-7 min. Add minced garlic and sauté another 1 min until fragrant. Transfer to the cutting board with chicken.

Make the Sauce: In the same pot, melt butter and whisk in flour until lightly golden (1 1/2 min). Slowly whisk in chicken broth, lemon juice, salt, and pepper and whisk until smooth. Add half and half and bring to a simmer. Season the sauce with more salt and pepper to taste.

Combine the Casserole: Into the pasta pot, add the chicken, mushrooms mixture, parsley, and all of your sauce. Stir to combine. You can add 1/2 cup of reserved pasta water to make it saucier then spread it evenly into your prepared casserole dish.  Sprinkle the top generously with mozzarella.
To Bake: Cover with foil and bake at 350˚F for 20 min, then remove the lid and continue baking for 15 min. You can broil for the last 1-2 minutes to get some golden spots on top.

name: Chicken Salad
description: Craving that Chicken Salad Chick Classic Carol Chicken Salad? You’re in luck! No need for fancy ingredients—just tender white meat chicken, celery, mayo, and a hint of my secret seasoning blend. Make your own Chicken Salad Chick recipe right at home!

ingredients.name: shredded chicken (all-white meat or rotisserie chicken. Small shreds work best. See notes.)

instructions: Add the chicken, celery, mayonnaise, vinegar, and seasonings in a large mixing bowl. Stir until well combined.
Taste and season with additional salt and pepper if desired.
Allow the chicken salad to chill in the fridge for at least 30 minutes for optimal flavor. May be served immediately of course!

name: Chicken Parmesan
description: Classic crispy chicken breasts topped with marinara sauce and melted cheese—an Italian-American favorite. Rao's Sauce suggested with angel hair pasta.

ingredients.name: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
Place chicken breasts between plastic wrap and pound to even thickness. Season with salt and pepper.
Prepare three bowls: flour, beaten eggs, and a mixture of bread crumbs + Parmesan.
Dredge chicken in flour, dip in eggs, then coat with breadcrumb mixture.
Heat olive oil in a pan and cook chicken until golden on both sides.
Transfer to a baking dish. Spoon tomato sauce over each piece and top with mozzarella, provolone, and basil.
Drizzle with olive oil.
Bake until cheese is melted and bubbly and chicken is cooked through (about 15–20 minutes).

name: Lady & Sons Beef Vegetable Soup
description: Favorite Beef veggie soup

ingredients.name: Beef bone
ingredients.prepNote: if don't have beef bone, get a couple of beef short ribs to add flavor

instructions: If using chuck roast, heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Place the roast in the skillet and cook until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side. Remove the roast from the skillet and cut it into 1 1/2 to 2-inch cubes; discard the fat. Place the beef cubes in a large stockpot. (If using short ribs, you can put them right in the pot with no preparation).

Add the water, seasonings, onion and tomatoes and bring to a boil over high heat. Cover the pot; reduce the heat so that the liquid simmers, and cook for 1 1/2 to 2 hours, or until the meat is very tender. If using short ribs, remove them from the pot and cut the meat from the bones, discard the bones and fat, and return the meat to the pot. Add the remaining vegetables and the macaroni and return the soup to a boil, stirring to distribute the ingredients. Reduce the heat and simmer for 45 minutes. Just before serving, add fresh parsley to pot. To remove excess fat from the surface of the soup, swirl a lettuce leaf around the surface-it will pick up a lot of the fat.
The chuck roast will yield more meat, but the bones from the short ribs give the soup an incredible flavor.

name: Beef tips with Stew Meat
description: tender and easy beef tips

ingredients.name: (.75 (.75 ounce) packet dry brown gravy mix

instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add onion and saute until almost translucent, about 3 minutes. Add stew meat and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides; about 3 to 5 minutes.

Pour 2 cups water, soy sauce, and Worcestershire sauce into the skillet. Stir in garlic powder, salt, and pepper; bring to a boil. Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer until meat is tender, 1 1/2 to 2 hours.

When the meat is almost finished, mix remaining 1 cup water and gravy mix until thoroughly combined; stir into the meat mixture and bring to a boil. Cook, stirring frequently, until slightly thickened.

name: Beef Tips and Gravy
description: Learn how to make Beef Tips and Gravy from scratch in one pot on the stove top or in the Slow Cooker! This classic comfort food is great with mashed potatoes, egg noodles, or rice.

ingredients.name: ½ teaspoon EACH: salt, garlic powder, onion powder, chili powder, paprika
ingredients.prepNote: EACH: salt, garlic powder, onion powder, chili powder, paprika

ingredients.name: ¼ teaspoon EACH: dried thyme, dried rosemary

instructions: Combine beef seasoning mix and measure out gravy ingredients before beginning.
Cut meat into 1-inch cubes, discard any large pieces of fat.
Combine beef seasoning and sprinkle it over the meat. Toss to coat. Sprinkle flour over the meat and toss again.

Heat 3 Tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the meat in batches, donât overcrowd the pan. Brown on each side for 30-45 seconds. Add a splash of more oil if needed. Transfer to a plate. Repeat with remaining beef. *Don't cook the beef all the way through. You want it to cook in the gravy to add more flavor.

Add ¼ cup wine and use a silicone spatula to âcleanâ (deglaze) the bottom of the pan, removing any brown remnants. The brown specks are called âfondâ and will give the sauce lots of good flavor.
Let the wine gently bubble and reduce by half. Reduce heat to medium and add the butter and the onions. Cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add all remaining gravy ingredients except the cornstarch mixture. Bring to a boil.
Combine the corn starch and cold water. Whisk into the sauce, a little at a time. Stir continuously. Continue until your desired consistency is obtained.
Reduce heat to medium-low and add the beef along with the juices from the plate. Cook with the lid partially covered for 10-15 minutes, until beef is cooked through. Remove from heat.
Optional: Swirl in 1 Tablespoon of COLD butter at the end, this is called âMonter au Beurreâ and it adds a smooth, velvety finish to sauces and gravies.
Serve with mashed potatoes, egg noodles, or over rice!

name: Beef Tenderloin
description: Anyone can make restaurant-quality Beef Tenderloin Roast from home and I& l walk you through it step-by-step! It;s easy, incredibly tender and full of flavor.

ingredients.name: -5 pound Beef tenderloin* (, trimmed and tied*)

instructions: Trim and Truss Meat (your butcher can do this, and tie it): Remove fat and any silver skin from the meat. Fold the tail (tapered-end) under and use kitchen twine to tie the meat in 1-inch sections. (Depending on your pan size, you may want to cut the beef tenderloin in half, so you have two equal size pieces to fit in a cast-iron skillet, Dutch-oven, roasting pan*, or on a baking sheet).

Air Chill Meat (Optional, but recommended): 10-24 hours before cooking, pat the meat dry with paper towels and season on all sides with kosher salt and black pepper. Place on a wire rack set over a lined baking sheet and allow to chill, uncovered, in the fridge for 10-24 hours. This simple step infuses flavor and will help yield a wonderful crust on the meat. If you donât have time, skip this step.
Make Herb Butter: Combine all ingredients and set aside until ready to use (can be made up to 2 weeks ahead, stored in the fridge).

Prepare Beef Tenderloin: Remove roast from the fridge 1 to 2 hours before cooking, to allow meat to come to room temperature and cook evenly. If you did not air chill the meat, pat the meat with paper towels all over, then season on all sides with salt and pepper.
Preheat oven to 425 degrees F.

Sear Meat: Heat oil in a large cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the prepared beef tenderloin, rotating it slowly to sear for a few minutes on each side, to create a golden-brown crust (about 10 minutes).
Brush herb butter all over the meat.

Oven: Transfer pan to the oven and roast, uncovered, for just 10-20 minutes, or until it reaches desired temperature: (Remove the meat about 5 degrees earlier than desired temperature, because it will continue to cook as it rests!)Rare: 115-120 degrees F (remove from oven around 110-115 degrees F)Medium rare 120-125 degrees (remove from oven when center is 115-120 degrees F)Medium 130-135F (remove from oven around 125-130 degrees F)
Rest covered with aluminum foil, for 15-20 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve: Remove twine and slice crosswise into 1-inch-thick rounds. Serve with whipped horseradish on the side, for dipping.

name: Beef Burgundy Over Noodles
description: I got this delightful beef burgundy recipe from my sister-in-law many years ago and have used it ever since. Whenever I serve it to guests, they always request this. The tender beef, mushrooms and flavorful sauce are delicious over noodles. âMargaret Welder, Madrid, Iowa

ingredients.name: beef top sirloin steak, cut into 1/4-inch-thick strips

instructions: In a Dutch oven or large nonstick skillet, heat butter over medium-high heat; saute beef and onion just until beef is lightly browned, 1-2 minutes. Stir in mushrooms, wine, 1/4 cup water, 2 tablespoons parsley and seasonings; bring to a boil. Reduce heat; simmer, covered, until beef is tender, about 1 hour.
Meanwhile, cook egg noodles according to package directions. Drain.

In a small bowl, mix flour and remaining water until smooth; stir into beef mixture. Bring to a boil; cook and stir until thickened, about 2 minutes. Discard bay leaf and clove. If desired, stir in browning sauce. Serve over noodles. Sprinkle with remaining parsley.
notes: Add bacon for sautéing if available

name: Italian Breaded Steak Recipe (make with Amagio Sauce - separate recipe)
description: This family favorite Italian Breaded Steak recipe of breaded and pan-seared beef is cooked until golden brown for the perfect weeknight meal.

ingredients.name: 8- ounce beef tenderloins I also used 2 10 ounce New York strip steaks
coarse salt and freshly cracked pepper to taste

instructions: Make the seasoned Italian breadcrumbs and briefly set them to the side in a large shallow bowl or cake tin.
Next, gently pound the steak on both sides between parchment or plastic wrap until it is roughly Â¾â to 1â thick.
Season the steak on both sides with salt and pepper.
Next, add 1/3 cup of olive oil to a large shallow bowl or cake tin.
Coat the seasoned steak in the olive oil and drain off any excess.

Add it to the bowl or tin of breadcrumbs and completely cover the steak on all sides and press the crumbs into it so that it is coated. Set it to the side on a sheet tray or platter and repeat the process until all the steak is breaded.
Add ¼ cup each of olive and avocado oil to a large non-stick skillet over medium heat and heat for 90 seconds.
Place the steaks into the hot oil and cook them untouched for 3 to 3 ½ minutes or until the bottom side is well browned.
Flip the steak over and cook for 2 to 3 minutes or until it reaches 120° to 125° internally, or until your desired internal temperature is achieved.
Serve the steak as is or over arugula with lemons. Try adding Amogio sauce over top.

name: Asian Ground Turkey with Rice
description: This Asian ground turkey recipe for dinner is quick, flavorful, and healthy. I bet it will be a hit at your house!

ingredients.name: washed and trimmed green beans or Chinese long beans if available

instructions: Start rice cooking in lightly salted water.

Stir in chili garlic sauce, hoisin sauce, and ginger. Add green beans and soy sauce, and stir to coat. Continue cooking over high heat for 7-9 minutes longer, stirring frequently, until beans are slightly tender.
Stir in rice vinegar, and cook one minute longer. Serve over cooked rice.

name: Chicken Fricassee with Apples
description: With its blend of sweet apples and savory chicken, this fricassee is just what you need for a comforting fall dinner.

ingredients.name: Â½ pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts (2 large), or chicken tenderloins (see note)
Honeycrisp, Fuji, or Gala apples, cored and cut into Â¼-inch (6-mm) thick slices (do not peel)
minced fresh thyme (or Â¾ teaspoon dried)

instructions: Slice the chicken breasts in half horizontally to form four flat fillets. (Skip this step if using chicken tenderloins; instead, using the palm of your hand, gently flatten the tenderloins so that they are an even Â½-inch/1-cm thickness.)

Pat the chicken dry with paper towels and season all over with 1 teaspoon salt and Â½ teaspoon pepper. Spread the flour in a shallow dish. Dredge the chicken in the flour to coat, shaking to remove excess; transfer to a plate and set aside.

Melt 1 tablespoon of the butter with 1 tablespoon of the oil in a 12-inch (30-cm) nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the apples to the pan and season with Â¼ teaspoon salt and Â¼ teaspoon pepper. Cook the apples, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned and softened, 6 to 8 minutes. Transfer to a second plate; set aside.

Melt the remaining 1 tablespoon butter with the remaining tablespoon oil in the now-empty skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook until lightly browned but not quite cooked through, about 2 minutes per side. Set the partially cooked chicken on another plate and set aside.
Add the onion, thyme, a heaping Â¼ teaspoon salt, and Â¼ teaspoon pepper to now-empty skillet and cook over medium heat until the onion is softened and browned, 4 to 5 minutes.
Add the cider and broth to the skillet, increase the heat to high, and bring the mixture to a boil. Cook, uncovered, until the liquid is reduced by approximately half, about 5 minutes.

Add the cream and bring to a boil. Cook until sauce has thickened slightly, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the chicken and any accumulated juices back to the skillet, reduce the heat to low, and simmer, uncovered, until the chicken is cooked through, 2 to 3 minutes.
Stir in the vinegar and apples. Taste the sauce and adjust the seasoning, if necessary. Spoon the sauce and apples over the chicken, sprinkle with the chives, and serve.

I buy two large chicken breasts and slice them in half horizontally, which makes four nice portions that cook quickly in one batch. I don't recommend using the pre-sliced chicken breasts sold at the supermarket â they are typically very thin, which means you'd need more of them to get 1Â½ pounds (680 g), and you'd have to cook the chicken in two batches. If you don't want to bother with slicing the chicken breasts yourself, chicken tenderloins are a great alternative.

name: The Best Chocolate Cake Recipe {Ever}
description: The Best Chocolate Cake Recipe - A one bowl chocolate cake recipe that is quick, easy, and delicious! Updated with gluten-free, dairy-free, and egg-free options!

ingredients.name: milk (or buttermilk, almond, or coconut milk)
vegetable oil (or canola oil, or melted coconut oil)

instructions: Preheat oven to 350Âº F. Prepare two 9-inch cake pans by spraying with baking spray, buttering and lightly flouring, or brushing them with homemade chocolate pan release.

Add flour, sugar, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda, salt and espresso powder to a large bowl or the bowl of a stand mixer. Whisk through to combine or, using your paddle attachment, stir through flour mixture until combined well.
Add milk, vegetable oil, eggs, and vanilla to flour mixture and mix together on medium speed until well combined. Reduce speed and carefully add boiling water to the cake batter until well combined.
Distribute cake batter evenly between the two prepared cake pans. Bake for 30-35 minutes, until a toothpick or cake tester inserted in the center of the chocolate cake comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and allow to cool for about 10 minutes, remove from the pan and cool completely.
Frost the cake with Chocolate Buttercream Frosting.

name: Eye Round
description: Poor Man's Prime Rib creates a tasty celebration for milestones, ideal for sharing.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 500 degrees F.
In a large roasting pan or baking dish, add the roast beef and season it with the garlic powder, the onion powder, the salt, and the black pepper. Do not cover the roast.
Place the roast in the oven uncovered and reduce the heat to 475 degrees F.
Bake the roast beef for about 7 minutes per pound, about 21 minutes for a 3-pound roast.
Turn the oven off but do not open the door at all.

Let the roast sit in the off, closed oven until the meat reaches an internal temperature of 145 degrees F for medium, about 2 hours 30 minutes. Again, do not open the oven door at any time during this process before the 2 hour 30 minute mark to check the temperature, even a little.
Once at temperature, transfer the roast from the oven to a cutting board with a sharp knife and carve it into thin slices.
Plate and serve.

name: Baked Artichoke Chicken in White Wine Sauce
description: In this gluten-free twist on classic chicken piccata, chicken cutlets bake in the oven until juicy and swimming in a rich white wine sauce. Canned artichokes add extra tang and texture with next to no effort, and the chicken is baked so there's no need to bread or fry! Serve this weeknight-friendly one pan wonder with a big salad and rice or couscous.

ingredients.name: to 2 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts

instructions: Get ready: Preheat your oven to 425Â°F.

Turn the chicken breasts into cutlets so they cook quickly and more evenly. Use a sharp knife to cut each chicken breast horizontally into two halves, starting on the thicker end and carefully slicing your way to the thinner end. Place the chicken breast halves, one at a time, in a large bag and zip the top shut (make sure to release the air before closing the bag). Use a meat mallet or the back of a heavy cast iron skillet to pound the chicken flat. You want each breast to be about 1/4-inch thick all over.

Season the chicken. In a small bowl, mix together the oregano, thyme, paprika, and a large pinch of salt and pepper (about 1/2 to 3/4 teaspoons each). Pat the chicken dry and season on both sides with the spice mixture.

Make the sauce. In a large oven-safe pan with a lid, melt the butter over medium-high heat. Add the olive oil and wait for it to shimmer. Add the garlic and cook, stirring regularly, until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Now add the wine and cook briefly to reduce by half, then add the lemon juice and capers. Turn the heat off.

Bake. Add the chicken and artichoke hearts to the pan and spoon some of the sauce on top. Cover the pan and transfer to middle rack of your heated oven. Bake covered for about 10 minutes, then uncover and bake for another 5 to 8 minutes or until the chicken breast are fully cooked through and no longer pink in the middle.
Finish and serve. Garnish with the parsley and lemon zest and serve immediately.

name: Chicken and Broccoli (Chinese Takeout Style)
description: An easy chicken and broccoli stir fry recipe that yields juicy chicken and crisp broccoli in a rich brown sauce, just like the one at a Chinese restaurant.
instructions: Slice the chicken against the grain into thin bite-size pieces, no thicker than 1/4â (1/2 cm), transfer into a medium-size bowl. Add the marinade ingredients. Stir to mix well. Let sit for 10 minutes while preparing other ingredients.
Combine all the sauce ingredients in a bowl. Stir to mix well.

Bring 1/3 cup of water to a boil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the broccoli and cover. Steam until the broccoli just turns tender and the water evaporates, about 40 to 50 seconds. Transfer the broccoli to a plate. Wipe the pan with a paper towel held in a pair of tongs if thereâs any water left.

Add the oil and swirl to coat the bottom. Spread the chicken in the skillet in a single layer. Allow to cook without touching for 30 seconds, or until the bottom side is browned. Flip to cook the other side for a few seconds. Stir and cook until the surface is lightly charred and the inside is still pink.
Add the garlic and ginger. Stir a few times to release the flavor and fragrance.

Return the broccoli to the pan. Stir the sauce again to dissolve the cornstarch completely and pour it into the skillet. Cook and stir until the sauce thickens, about 1 minute. Transfer everything to a plate immediately.
Serve hot with steamed rice or boiled noodles as a main dish.

name: Maple Chicken Breakfast Sausage â Low Carb, Paleo, Gluten Free
description: Good breakfast sausage

ingredients.name: Tbs. Maple Syrup - (Low Carb Sugar-Free Option - Here)

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground chicken, maple syrup, salt, rubbed sage, parsley, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper, thyme, red pepper flakes, nutmeg, and cloves.
Mix until all ingredients are well-incorporated
Form into 2 oz. patties. I like to roll them into balls and then flatten them into patties.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Lightly oil the skillet.
Once the oil is heated, place the sausage patties in the pan. Cook until browned on both sides and cooked all the way through.
Serve and enjoy!!

name: Julia Child Beef Stroganoff Recipe
description: Julia Child Beef Stroganoff is a classic, sophisticated dish featuring seared beef tenderloin strips in a rich sauce of mustard, cream, and mushrooms. It creates a velvety, savory dinner that is surprisingly quick to make but tastes elegant enough for company.

ingredients.name: Tb minced shallots or green onions
Â½ lbs beef tenderloin, trimmed
ingredients.prepNote: Can also use Costco cap-off sirloin

ingredients.name: Â¼ cup Madeira (or dry white vermouth)

instructions: Brown the mushrooms:Â Heat butter and oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and sautÃ© 4-5 minutes until lightly browned.
Add shallots:Â Stir in shallots and cook 1 minute more. Season with salt and pepper. Transfer to a side dish.
Step 2: Prep and Sear the Beef
Slice the beef:Â Cut tenderloin into pieces about 2 inches across and Â½ inch thick. Dry thoroughly with paper towels.

Sear in batches:Â Heat butter and oil in the same skillet. When the foam subsides, add beef in a single layer. Don't crowd the pan. Sear 2-3 minutes per side. The goal is brown exterior, rosy red interior.
Set aside:Â Transfer beef to a plate. Discard the fat from the skillet.
Deglaze:Â Add Madeira and stock to the skillet. Boil rapidly, scraping up the browned bits, until reduced to about â cup.
Add cream:Â Beat in the whipping cream, then the cornstarch mixture. Simmer for 1 minute.
Add mushrooms:Â Return the mushrooms to the sauce and simmer 1 minute more. The sauce should be lightly thickened. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Return the beef:Â Season the beef lightly with salt and pepper. Add it back to the skillet along with any juices.
Warm gently:Â Cover and heat to just below a simmer for 3-4 minutes. Be very careful not to overdo it or the beef will be well done rather than rare.
Finish with butter:Â Off heat, swirl in the softened butter. Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.
notes:
Category: Dinner,Main
Notes: cook meat in batches and don't crowd the pan. 
I like to serve over rice and usually make green beans or asparagus

name: Baked Ziti
description: Yummy pasta dish, great for a crowd

ingredients.name: Sweet Italian Sausage
ingredients.prepNote: brown and drain on paper towels in a bowl

ingredients.name: Pasta noodles
ingredients.prepNote: I use either rigatoni, ziti or penne noodles

ingredients.name: Shredded Parmesan
ingredients.prepNote: I don't measure, just sprinkle on each layer, to taste

ingredients.name: Shredded Mozzarella
ingredients.prepNote: I don't measure, just sprinkle on each layer, to taste

ingredients.name: Fresh Basil
ingredients.prepNote: chopped and sprinkle on each layer

instructions: Brown the ground beef in a large saucepan,  drain and hold to the side.
Brown the Italian sausage, drain on paper towels in a bowl.
Put meat back in pot and then add the 2 jars of Prego. Add sugar and let this simmer for 2 hours or so.
After sauce has cooked for a couple hours or so, boil noodles and chop the basil. Drain the noodles and set aside. Turn oven on to 425

Spoon about a cup of the sauce on the bottom of a 9 x 13-inch (23 x 33-cm) baking dish. Layer 1/3 of the noodles and spread 1c or so of sauce over the noodles. Sprinkle with shredded mozzarella (~1 cup/113 g) and the parmesan, sprinkle some basil. Then repeat this process two more times. Transfer to the oven and bake, uncovered, until the cheese has melted and browned, 15 to 20 minutes. Sprinkle with more basil and serve.
